sections.paragraph:
with suff. סִתְרִי.-

(1) a hiding; hence something secret, clandestine, hidden, Jud. 3:19 , דְּבַר־סֵתֶר “some secret thing;” 1Sa 25:20, סֵתֶר הָהָר “the covert of the mountain.” לֶחֶס סְתָרִיס “bread to be eaten in secret,” Pro 9:17. With prefixes, בַּסֵּתֶר secretly, privately, 1Sa 19:2 2Sa 12:12 Job 13:10 31:27 Pro 21:14, and so frequently.

(2) specially a vail, covering (Arab. سَتْرُ, Syr. ܣܶܢܬܪܳܐ a vail, a curtain), Job 22:14 24:15 Psa 81:8, בְּסֵתֶר רַעַם “in the covering of thunder,” in the clouds replete with thunderings; Psa 18:12.

(3) protection, defence, Psa 27:5 32:7 61:5 91:1 119:114 Isa 32:2.
